steps.title: Use the procedure context
steps.content: The passage says that before 1917, the Senate had no formal measure that could be used to end excessively prolonged debate. The blank must mean officially call on or activate a rule.

steps.title: Choose the formal verb
steps.content: Choice A means made up on the spot, which conflicts with a formal measure. Choice C means eliminated, but the Senate needed a measure to use, not destroy. Choice D means divided into parts, which does not fit ending a debate. The remaining verb means officially put a procedure into effect. The answer is B.
